message:
feat(cass): Cx-first search_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extends the Cx-first migration to CassClient (cross-agent session
search wrapper), mirroring the caut.rs pattern landed in commit
151d689c. The subprocess timeout on the `cass` CLI invocation now
binds to a caller-supplied Cx so cancellation, budget, and virtual
time propagate into the external process lifecycle.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/cass.rs (+96 src, +108 tests)

   a. `CassClient::search_with_cx(cx, query, options) -> ...`

      New Cx-first entry point. Delegates to the internal
      `run_with_cx` which uses `runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx`
      instead of the ambient `timeout`.

      Legacy `search()` under `asupersync-runtime` now delegates
      to `search_with_cx` with `cx::for_request()`. Non-asupersync
      fallback preserves the inline timeout() call.

   b. `CassClient::run_with_cx(cx, args) -> Result<String, _>`

      Cx-first internal helper. Subprocess timeout is bound via
      `timeout_with_cx(cx, ...)`. Shares `finalize_output()` with
      the legacy `run()` so exit-status / stderr-preview /
      output-too-large checks stay bit-for-bit identical between
      the two paths.

   c. `build_search_args(query, options) -> Vec<String>`

      Extracted as a pure function so `search` and `search_with_cx`
      build argv identically. Now unit-testable without a real
      subprocess.

   d. `finalize_output(&self, Output) -> Result<String, _>`

      Extracted shared post-timeout validation. Unified between
      the Cx-first and ambient paths.

2. Four new tests under `mod tests`:

   * `build_search_args_minimal_options`: default options yield
     exactly `["search", query, "--robot"]`.

   * `build_search_args_all_options_populated`: all seven options
     set; asserts presence of each flag+value pair.

   * `build_search_args_with_only_limit`: one-option happy path.

   * `search_with_cx_returns_io_error_for_invalid_binary` (asupersync
     only): invalid binary path surfaces an IO-shaped error, never
     Timeout or NonZeroExit. Pins the error-surface contract so a
     future refactor doesn't flip invalid-binary into one of the
     other error surfaces.

3. Import gate: `runtime_compat::timeout` is now
   `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` since the
   asupersync-runtime path goes through `timeout_with_cx` in
   `run_with_cx`. Avoids the `-D unused-imports` clippy lint.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cass::: 65/65 pass
    (61 existing + 4 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Only the `search` path is migrated in this commit. The other five
public CassClient async methods (search_sessions, query_session,
query, status, trigger_index) all go through the same internal
`run` which now delegates to `run_with_cx` under asupersync-runtime,
so they implicitly get the same timeout_with_cx binding through the
ambient Cx path. Exposing explicit _with_cx variants for the
remaining five is a straightforward mechanical follow-up.

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
